message:
fix(concurrent): fail-closed on missing MVCC session and harden SSI test resilience

Two safety improvements to the concurrent transaction subsystem:

1. Connection commit planning (connection.rs):
   - Change concurrent commit behavior when the MVCC session ID is
     missing: previously returned Ok(None) which silently skipped all
     FCW/SSI conflict validation, allowing potentially conflicting
     writes to commit unchecked. Now returns an Internal error with
     "rollback required" message, forcing the caller to abort rather
     than bypass the safety net.
   - Add test_commit_fails_when_concurrent_session_is_missing() to
     verify the fail-closed invariant.
   - Add test_concurrent_deposit_sum_invariant_probe_bd_rjc(): 8-worker
     concurrent deposit test (240 txns/worker, 64 accounts) verifying
     that the final SUM(balance) equals initial_sum + committed_delta,
     catching any lost-update or phantom-write anomalies under real
     contention.

2. SSI serialization E2E test (bd_3plop_5):
   - Replace rollback_best_effort() with rollback_required() that
     returns errors instead of silently swallowing them, ensuring
     rollback failures are captured in hard_failures.
   - Add connection reopen after transient errors: when a retry is
     needed, open a fresh connection to avoid operating on a connection
     in an indeterminate state after a failed COMMIT.
   - Extract open_worker_connection() helper for consistent PRAGMA
     configuration (busy_timeout + concurrent_mode) across worker
     connection lifecycle.
   - Consolidate two separate UPDATE statements for account transfers
     into a single CASE-based UPDATE, reducing the write-set footprint
     and eliminating the window for partial-transfer anomalies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
